title: Calorophus laxiflorus
taxonomy: Calorophus laxiflorus is a species of flowering plant in the family Phrygiopidae. It is a type of hummingbird flower, which is a common name for plants in the genus Calorophus. Calorophus laxiflorus is native to Mexico and belongs to the order Asparagales. The plant is characterized by its tubular flowers, which attract hummingbirds as pollinators. In terms of its classification, Calorophus laxiflorus is a member of the subfamily Phragmidoideae.
uses: Calorophus laxiflorus is a plant species with traditional medicinal uses, particularly in Latin American folk medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are utilized to treat various health issues. Traditionally, the plant is used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. The plant's leaves and roots are often used to prepare teas, infusions, and poultices for medicinal purposes. However, the efficacy and safety of these uses have not been extensively scientifically validated.
